title: Noah Kahan joins Fenway Music Hall of Fame after historic four-night run
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Noah Kahan was inducted into the Fenway Music Hall of Fame following his four-night concert run at Fenway Park.
Kahan joins artists like Billy Joel and Paul McCartney as the fourth inductee in the Hall of Fame.
Kahan's Fenway performances featured fireworks and appearances by sports figures Rob Gronkowski and David Ortiz.

title: AG: Mass. to receive $730,000 from Cash App in alleged deception settlement
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts will receive $730,000 from Cash App as part of a $45 million settlement over alleged deceptive practices.
Attorney General Andrea Campbell stated that Cash App misled consumers about safety and failed to protect users from fraud.
The settlement requires Cash App to improve security measures, provide live customer support, and refund users for unauthorized transactions.

title: Tyngsborough man may face charges after allegedly taking police boat, posing as officer
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 63-year-old Tyngsborough man allegedly operated a police boat with emergency lights activated at Lake Mascuppic.
The man reportedly attempted to enforce local boating rules, telling several people to leave the lake.
Tyngsborough police plan to issue a summons for him on a charge of impersonating a police officer.

title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 60 international tall ships and military vessels from 20 countries docked in Boston Harbor for Sail Boston 250.
The USS Constitution led the Parade of Sail in Boston Harbor, attracting thousands of visitors to the city.
The event marks the final stop of the Sail250 tour, with ships arriving from New York for the six-day celebration.

title: Red Sox draft SS Jake Schaffner with No. 20 pick
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox drafted shortstop Jake Schaffner from UNC with the No. 20 pick in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Schaffner, ranked 75th by MLB.com, hit .356 with six home runs and 50 RBI in 68 games at UNC.
Known for speed and defense, Schaffner stole 26 bases and committed only three errors at shortstop this season.

title: Sail Boston 2026: The Grand Parade of Sail is rooted in rich history and tradition in the Commonwealth
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: The Sail Boston 2026 parade, led by the USS Constitution, featured historic ships from around the world.
The event marked Boston's 250th anniversary, highlighting maritime history with replica schooners like Bluenose Two and Spirit of Bermuda.
Sail Boston is a free, multi-generational event fostering appreciation for maritime heritage among attendees.

title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Brayan Bello, of the Boston Red Sox, was named midseason AL 'Cy Yuk' winner by The Athletic for a 10.35 ERA.
Bello posted 41 earned runs over 35.2 innings in eight starts, leading to his demotion to Triple-A Worcester.
Despite poor starting stats, Bello excelled in relief with a 0.71 ERA over 25.1 innings for the Red Sox.

title: Red Sox face all too familiar question heading into MLB trade deadline: Buy or sell?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Red Sox are on a seven-game winning streak, two games shy of the AL wild-card spot.
Boston previously planned to sell key players like Aroldis Chapman and Sonny Gray due to poor performance.
Craig Breslow faces pressure to decide on buying or selling at the trade deadline amid team improvements.

title: Review & Setlist: Shakira delivers high-energy nostalgia and new beginnings at TD Garden
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Shakira performed at TD Garden as part of her Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour on July 10, 2026.
The concert featured a mix of nostalgic hits and new songs from her Grammy-winning album 'Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran'.
Highlights of the performance included visually elaborate sets, various dance styles, and a heartfelt tribute to her sons.

title: Healey announces tax credit awards to build almost 700 new homes in six Mass. cities
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Healey-Driscoll administration awards $18.6 million in tax credits for 662 new homes in six Massachusetts Gateway Cities.
Funding targets historic building conversions in Fall River, Lawrence, Lowell, Pittsfield, Taunton, and Worcester.
Massachusetts initiatives aim to address housing shortages and revitalize downtown areas through Gateway Cities development.
